proposed_relations.description: Increased bus speeds up to 19% from offset bus lanes cited in project proposal

evidence_refs.source_quote: Increased bus speeds up to 19% Injuries decreased 24%

rationale: Evidence block p019_c0008 is on the Proposed Offset Bus Lane page within the 116th Street Bus & Pedestrian Priority Project document, citing Lexington Ave offset bus lane benefits as justification for the project.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Increased bus speeds up to 19% Injuries decreased 24%

quarantine.note: The cited evidence block p019_c0008 states 'Increased bus speeds up to 19% Injuries decreased 24%' but does not connect this metric to the 116th Street Bus & Pedestrian Priority Project. The metric's own payload description acknowledges it is 'from Lexington Ave example' — it represents results from a different project (Lexington Ave offset bus lanes), cited as supporting justification, not a measured outcome of the 116th St project. The has_metric relation requires the metric to be about the subject project; using another project's results as precedent does not make it a metric of this project. The evidence block text itself never mentions the 116th St project or establishes the connection.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Increased bus speeds up to 19% Injuries decreased 24%

quarantine.note: Same evidence block p019_c0008 and same issue. The 'Injuries decreased 24%' metric is from Lexington Ave offset bus lanes (as stated in the metric's own description), not from the 116th St project. proposed_relations.description: CB11 Public Safety & Transportation Committee meeting in June 2025 where the project updated proposal was presented

evidence_refs.source_quote: Community Board 11 Public Safety & Transportation Committee

rationale: The event evidence blocks (p001_c0002, p001_c0003) appear on page 1 of the project presentation, immediately following p001_c0001 which names '116 th Street Bus and Pedestrian Priority Project – Updated Proposal.' Page 1 explicitly names both the project and this meeting.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Community Board 11 Public Safety & Transportation Committee

quarantine.note: The cited evidence blocks p001_c0002 ('Community Board 11 Public Safety & Transportation Committee') and p001_c0003 ('June 2025') do not mention the 116th Street Bus & Pedestrian Priority Project or establish any connection to it. The rationale relies on co-occurrence — that these blocks appear on the same page as p001_c0001 which names the project — but p001_c0001 is not cited as evidence. Per the rule that 'co-occurrence in a document is not a relation,' the cited blocks must themselves state the connection between the two records. Committee name and date alone do not establish that this meeting was about the 116th St project.
